results.content:
top of it. So when you're building directly in v0, it's really primarily a front-end tool, but 
then you could use what you've built here in the front end and then add it to add a backend in 
another tool. Overall, I'm really impressed with the design and I think that it's smart that V0 is 
optimizing for being the best design tool. I think that the fact that it was able to take this style 
that we did in the beginning and basically you can change this to other design systems. So, just 
to show you like what cosmic knight looks like for example, it looks like this. So, you're going 
to get a different design system that's going to match what you want. You can also go and under 
design systems do new design system and change everything about this to make it exactly what [...] you want to customize what all of your projects in V0 look like. So to me the design system is 
the killer feature of V0. I wish they had more of them even for default design systems cuz that 
would give us even more customizable designs that we could add. So that's one thing that I would 
say. I guess it would be nice if you could build the back end directly in and maybe they'll add 
that in the future, but right now just building the front end I think is enough for this tool. 
But if you need to have that backend integrated, you're going to need to use a tool like Base44 
and Lovable. So that's the nuance there. If you just want design, I do think V0 is still a really 
great option. So I hope that you enjoy this video. Leave a comment below. Let me know what you [...] I click subscribe, it says no thank you page detected. Looks like there is an issue. Okay, so 
apparently it should be fixed now. Let's try it one more time. Let's see if we publish if that 
is able to fix it somehow. Okay, so it says it's ready. So now we can at least view the site and it 
really does look beautiful. I'm really impressed with the design that I'm seeing here. So overall 
the design of this homepage is great. Really happy with it. But we are running into some issues. So 
let's see now if this will take us. Okay, so now it is working. I don't know what was happening. 
I'll check it again in the designer real quick. But now we have this thank you page which looks 
great and that's what I was hoping for. It even has a referral link. It built out a lot of things

results.title: Design Systems 2.0 | v0 Docs
results.content:
Take the review seriously. v0 saves this starter into the skill, and every app you build with the design system later starts from it. Check both how it looks and how it's wired up: components and tokens, providers, theme setup, fonts, and global styles. If anything is off, even a small detail, ask v0 to fix it in the chat and re-check before you approve. A correct starter means every future app begins from a working foundation instead of inheriting the same issue each time.

Once you approve, your design system lives in the saved skill, ready to use anywhere. The chat where you imported it is only the starting point — attach the skill in a new chat or keep building in this one.

## Use a saved design system

Saved skills appear on the Design Systems page under Skills. [...] Ask v0 to update the skill in a chat. Include the new version, changed source, release notes, migration guide, and any breaking changes. v0 updates the saved skill and verifies the starter app again.

### Can v0 use private packages?

Yes. Add package credentials such as `NPM_TOKEN` as Development shared environment variables during import. You need at least the Developer role on Vercel to use them in v0. If that is not possible, attach `.tgz` package archives.

### Will existing projects automatically update when the skill changes?

No. Updating a skill changes the reusable instructions and starter for future work. For existing projects, ask v0 to update the app code with the latest version of the skill.

### Who can create, edit, or delete a design system skill? [...] ```

results.title: Introducing design systems in v0
results.content:
401 views

### Replies

Best

Vatsal M

This looks like an upgrade to our prototyping process for new SAAS apps. I can now choose across so many colour options for my apps, YAY!  
  
But, for it to be a full on design system it should also allow us to tweak things other than color values like border styles, typography, spacings, grid system, etc. Currently it gives us ability to test our app UI in various color palettes very quickly which is great but having the other design capabilities would make it a complete 'design system' system.

results.title: v0 by Vercel: Full stack vibe coding platform. Created ...
results.content:
#### What's great

v0 turns a rough idea into a working UI in minutes. I describe what I want, get clean React and Tailwind back, and ship it instead of fighting boilerplate. The output is close enough to production that the edits are small, and the iteration loop is fast enough that I stay in flow instead of stalling on layout. It's now my default first pass for new pages.

#### What needs improvement

It can over-assume structure when the prompt is vague, so you get further by iterating in small steps than asking for everything at once. Tighter control over reusing an existing design system and component library would help on

larger projects, so new output matches what is already there instead of needing manual alignment.

#### vs Alternatives

Lovable
bolt.new [...] #### vs Alternatives

Lovable
bolt.new

Generic chat models give you code but no real design sense, and you spend the time wiring it into a stack yourself. v0 wins because it is built around React and Tailwind, the components drop straight into a Next.js project, and

the design quality out of the box is higher. Less cleanup, faster to something I would actually ship.

Karimuddin CS

#### What's great

I loved v0 to generate landing pages, but as a software architect, I like a bit of thoroughness and polished output. Which is fine; for free landing page generation from a single prompt, it beats many so-called industry leaders in this space. Solid product.

#### What needs improvement [...] #### What needs improvement

I still assume it improved for the backend integration. Which is ironic since Vercel is one of the most democratized platforms for complex delivery and release management.

#### vs Alternatives

It is free and UI/UX is almost on point, if you oput the right template in.

Remi Simmons

#### What's great

One of two options I use for frontend design and sites that don't require much backend. Can't wait for them to connect Convex then v0 will solidify my first stop.

#### What needs improvement

More Webflow and motion animation would be nice.

#### vs Alternatives

Replit
Lovable

I still use Lovable sometimes and probably more since they connected Supabase, this just make setting up my database way too easy. That's the feature that keeps me split.
